element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Upgradeable to level 5 via collecting numbered circles
Allows ship to sustain two hits instead of one; enables deeper weapon progression
Area-specific fire enemies (cigarettes/matchboxes in JP, petrol containers in US)
Requires player to learn trajectory control

element_introduction_order.element: Bomb Types: F, T, B, W
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Limited supply; upgradeable but consume double ammo when leveled
Used in inter-level shop for purchases; found randomly or from destroyed enemies
Collecting multiples in sequence upgrades weapons; limited to 5 levels per weapon
Regional variant: cigarettes/matchboxes (JP) vs. petrol containers (US)
Matchbox value pack (JP) or robotic variant (US); pattern memorization required

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Area 8 - Planet of the Sun Interior
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Multi-phase battle; first form differs between regions (baby-like in JP, grotesque in US)

typical_relief_triggers: Inter-level shop screen with money spending
typical_tension_triggers: Dense enemy waves with varied projectile patterns
Area-themed enemy types forcing weapon adaptation
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation through eight thematic planetary areas with inter-level shop breaks; climax in Area 8 two-part final boss battle

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Area 0 (hidden stage) accessible via Sound Test track 5 selection before stage choice

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like introduction with standard gun and basic enemy patterns in Areas 0-1; wing upgrade available early to reduce punishment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Weapon specialization required per area theme (fire weapons for Area 2, water mechanics for Area 3, etc.); mid-boss encounters introduce pattern memorization; money management becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Areas 6-7 (Earth/Sun planets) intensify enemy density and projectile complexity; Area 8 interior introduces final boss with multiple forms; regional content differences affect late-game pacing
notable_spikes: Area 2 mid-boss transition (first major skill gate)
Area 8 final boss first form (grotesque/baby variant demands aggressive strategy)
Area 8 final boss second form (extended pattern sequence)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each area (1-8) based on day-of-week element progression: Moon (Area 1) → Fire (Area 2) → Water (Area 3) → Wood (Area 4) → Metal/Money (Area 5) → Earth (Area 6) → Sun (Area 7) → Sun Interior (Area 8). Enemies and mid-bosses visually reinforce elemental context
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Area 2 (Planet of Fire) vs Area 3 (Planet of Water) enemy type variations

notable_level_patterns.description: Money collected during stage is spent between levels to upgrade weapons and bombs; provides tension release and strategic planning moment
Japanese version contains longer narrative opening/ending and different enemy designs (cigarettes, lighters); US version replaces with industrial/petroleum objects for cultural appropriateness
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Area 2 fire-themed enemies; Area 8 final boss first form

notable_level_patterns.description: Area 8 concludes with two-form boss battle; first form (grotesque/baby variant by region), second form continues challenge; defeats common shmup convention of single-phase finale
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Area 8 - Planet of the Sun Interior

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Gun-Nac article)
